conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do! I have one child, Alex, who's 9 years old and lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's great that you have Alex living with you.

To help determine your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask a few more questions.

Is Alex under 16 years old, or if they're 16 or over, are they in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is only 9, so he's well under 16.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that.

Next, I need to ask: Are you responsible for Alex? This means do you live with Alex and are you the person who is mainly responsible for their day-to-day care (such as providing food, clothing, and ensuring their wellbeing)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. Alex lives with me and I'm the one who looks after him day-to-day - I provide all his food, clothes, and take care of everything he needs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you for confirming.

Now I need to ask about your residency status: Do you normally live in the UK, and are you either a UK citizen or do you have the right to reside in the UK (for example, through settled status, pre-settled status, or another immigration status that allows you to claim benefits)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK and I'm a UK citizen, so no issues there.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for confirming that.

Just a couple more questions: Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex? For example, Alex's other parent or another person?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. It's just me looking after him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information.

One final question: Are there any circumstances that might affect Alex's care arrangements? For example:
- Is Alex in local authority care or being looked after by a local authority?
- Does Alex live in any form of residential care or institution?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nothing like that. Alex just lives at home with me full-time. He's not in any kind of care or residential home or anything.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 9 years old (under 16)
- You are responsible for Alex and he lives with you full-time
- You provide his day-to-day care
- You're a UK citizen living in the UK
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- Alex is not in local authority or residential care

You meet all the requirements for Child Benefit. You can now proceed to make a claim if you haven't already done so. Child Benefit is usually paid every 4 weeks, and the current rate depends on whether Alex is your only child or if you have other children.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 9 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex

circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) Alex is 9 years old, which is under 16 years old, meeting the age requirement; (2) The claimant is responsible for Alex, living with him and providing day-to-day care including food, clothing, and general wellbeing; (3) The claimant is a UK citizen and normally lives in the UK, satisfying residency requirements; (4) No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex; (5) Alex is not in local authority care or residential care and lives at home full-time with the claimant. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 9), who lives with them full-time. The claimant is a UK citizen living in the UK, is responsible for Alex's day-to-day care, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is not in any form of local authority or residential care. All eligibility requirements for Child Benefit are satisfied.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) Alex is 9 years old, which is under 16 years old, meeting the age requirement; (2) The claimant is responsible for Alex, living with him and providing day-to-day care including food, clothing, and general wellbeing; (3) The claimant is a UK citizen and normally lives in the UK, satisfying residency requirements; (4) No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex; (5) Alex is not in local authority care or residential care and lives at home full-time with the claimant. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 9), who lives with them full-time. The claimant is a UK citizen living in the UK, is responsible for Alex's day-to-day care, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is not in any form of local authority or residential care. All eligibility requirements for Child Benefit are satisfied.
